texts.full_text: between admiration for celebrities is blurred by literal ingestion of their essence—a disturbing projection into our own culture’s fascination. This exploration delves deep with unsettling imagery: a young man obsessed with his celebrity crush develops symptoms akin to her recent illness; another uses synthetic substances containing bits from deceased stars, aiming at closeness through physical assimilation. Cron伯格 paints this world as an exaggerated but not impossible future of our current culture's trajectory. The film’s chilling narrative is delivered in the same style that has made DavId Cronenberg famous: a blend of science fiction and body-horror elements with psychological depth—though, unlike his father who often leaves much to interpretation. Here Brandon offers more direct messages about celebrity worship being toxic or even deadly; he doesn't shy away from presenting it as grotesque. Ultimately "Antivirus" is an unsettling reflection on our culture's unhealthy fascination and obsession with celebrities that can lead us into dangerous, dystopian futures if left unchecked. It serves not only to entertain but also provoke thought about the consequences of such behavior—a call for self-awareness in a society increasingly driven by media-fueled celebrity worship. This is an unflinching critique on our modern obsession with celebrities and what it could lead us into, wrapped within Brandon CronenBerg's unique blend of horror that honors his father’s legacy while carving out its own space. texts.full_text: List of school districts in Texas#ReferencesExternal linksCategory:Schools in Texas Category:Education in Terry County, Texas The district is a part of the Region XVI Education Service Center. It serves approximately 500 students from kindergarten through grade twelve with an average enrollment at each campus being around fifty-five to sixty students. Wellman-Union High School offers several extracurricular activities including football (6-man), basketball, volleyball and cheerleading teams as well as various clubs such as FFA, National Honor Society and Student Council. The district also has a strong academic program that includes Advanced Placement classes for grades 9 to 12. The school's mascot is the Cougar with its colors being black, white, red gold respectively representing strength courage unity excellence leadership respect honor trust pride loyalty commitment service citizenship character integrity honesty responsibility creativity and determination among other qualities. The district has a strong community involvement through parent teacher organizations booster clubs local business partnerships support from area churches civic groups volunteer programs fundraising efforts donation drives student led initiatives such as blood drive food collection campaigns etc. Wellman-Union Independent School District is committed to providing its students with the highest quality education possible in an environment that promotes academic achievement character dEvelopment leadership skills citizenship responsibilities social responsibility community service personal growth and lifelong learning. The district believes strongly in promoting excellence

texts.full_text: Exercise and insulin stimulate skeletal muscle glucose transport through different mechanisms. This study was designed to examine the effects of acute exercise, insulin stimulation, and their combination on the kinetics of glucose transport in rat skeletal muscle. Sarcolemmal (SL) membranes were isolated from control (C), acute exercise (E), insulin-stimulated (I), and combined (E + I) rats. Michaelis-Menten kinetics indicated that the Vmax for glucose trtransport was significantly increased in E, I, and E + I groups compared to C. However, the Km value, which represents the affinity of the transporter for glucose, remained unchanged across all groups. Related Art As described in Japanese Laid-Open Patent Publication No. 2011-82624, for example, an ultrasonic transducer element chip is provided with a substrate. A plurality of openings are formed in the substrate. An ultrasonic transducer element is formed in each of the openings. The ultrasonic transducer elements are connected to a circuit board through connecting wires. This configuration allows for effective transmission and reception of ultrasonic waves, which is essential for applications such as medical imaging. The related art has explored various methods to enhance the performance and integration of ultrasonic transducer elements. For instance, advancements have been made in the materials used for the substrate and the transducer elements themselves, aiming to improve sensitivity and bandwidth. Additionally, techniques for reducing the size of the transducer elements while maintaining or enhancing their performance have been a focus. However, there are challenges associated with the current designs, such as the complexity of connecting multiple transducer elements to a circuit board, potential signal interference, and limitations in the miniaturization of components. texts.full_text: has key perceptual functions. It has been proposed that the location of the perirhinal cortex, near the end of a hierarchical sequence of visual (and other sensory) processing areas, reflects its importance for the categorization of complex visual objects ([@c12]; [@c20]; [@c24]; [@c36]; but see [@c30]). Consequently, it has been argued that some or even all of the apparent mnemonic deficits associated with perirhinal cortex damage in animals are, in fact, essentially perceptual deficits ([@c17]; [@c21]; [@c37]; [@c38]). One influential description of perirhinal cortex function that emphasizes its perceptual contributions is the perceptual-mnemonic/feature conjunction model ([@c18], [@c20]). In this model, the perirhinal cortex represents conjunctions of stimulus features, and the features are, themselves, represented in more caudal brain regions (e.g., areas V4 and TEO in the monkey). This model and closely related views of perirhinal cortex function (e.g., [@c24]) predict that the perirhinal cortex is important for solving those visual problems where the representation or recognition task requires combining multiple features to identify an object. For example, distinguishing between two similar objects based on a combination of color and shape would rely heavily on such feature conjunction abilities. The perceptual-mnemonic/feature conjunction model suggests that while damage to perirhinal cortex might impair the ability to recognize previousl

texts.full_text: {#Sec14} ------------------------------------------------------- T cells were purified from spleens of WT and *Gpr174* KO mice, mechanically disrupted over a 75 μm filter, and subjected to magnetic bead enrichment for CD4^+^CD25^+^ T cells using CD4^+^CD25^+^ Treg isolation kit (Miltenyi Biotec, Bergisch Gladbach, Germany). Enriched CD4^+^CD25^+^ T cells were counted and resuspended in PBS. A cell suspension containing 1 × 10^6^ per 100 μl PBS was injected into C57BL/6 *Rag2*^−/−^ mouse via tail vein. Histological analysis {#Sec15} --------------------- Lung tissue samples were fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde and tissue sections were stained with H&E stain. Lung damage examination was analyzed by tissue injury scoring system, as described previously^[@CR34]^. Cytokine analysis {#Sec16} ----------------- Serum cytokine levels of IL-1β, IL-6, TNF-α, and IL-10 were measured by ELISA kit (eBioscience) according to the manufacturer's protocol. Differentiation of bone marrow-derived macrophages {#Sec17} -------------------------------------------------- Bone marrow cells were isolated from femurs and tibias of WT and *Gpr174* KO mice. Differentiation of Bone Marrow-Derived Macrophages was induced by culturing bone marrow cells in MEM-F12 medium supplemented with 10% FBS and macrophage-colony stimulating factor (20 ng/ml M-CSF, proteintech, Rosemont, PA, USA). Bone Marrow-Derived Macrophages were cultured for 7 days with one renewal of culture medium on the third day. In vitro macrophage suppression assay {#Sec18} ------------------------------------- WT mice Marrow-Derived macrophages were resuspended in RPMI containing 10% FBS. CD4^+^CD25^+^ Treg cells and Marrow-Derived macrophages were seeded on 24-well plate in a 1:2 ratio. Macrophages were cultured alone or co-cultured with Treg cells for 40 h in the presence of 0.4 μg/ml anti-CD3 mAb (UCHT1, eBioscience), then were stimulated with 100 ng/ml LPS for 24 h. Supernatants were harvested for ELISA assay. Macrophages were digested with 0.25% trypsin for flow cytometry. Statistical analysis {#Sec19} -------------------- SPSS (version 19.0, SPSS Inc., Chicago, IL, USA) software was used to analyze data by two-tailed unpaired student's *t* test or one-way analysis of variance test. Data are presented as mean ± SD. *P* \< 0.05 was considered statistically significant (\**P* \< 0.05; \*\**P* \< 0.01). texts.full_text: studying the action of topoisomerase I targeted drugs. Almost 30 years after the unsuccessful clinical evaluation of camptothecin sodium, there has been a revival in interest in this class of agent that poisons topoisomerase I. Currently there are four camptothecin analogues in clinical trials each at different levels of advancement. Clinical data suggest that patterns of antitumor activity and toxicity profiles differ between analogues. In preclinical models antitumor activity appears to be highly schedule-dependent. Here we review rodent and human tumor models used in evaluation of efficacy, and models used to predict toxicities of these compounds. The major limitation of rodent models is that the mous are not predictive of human toxicity. In addition, the lack of a suitable human xenograft model has limited the ability to evaluate the efficacy of these agents in vivo. The development of a suitable human xenograft model would be a major step forward in the evaluation

texts.full_text: ve observed in my practice that often the assistant exerts considerable pressure on the patient\'s neck under the erroneous impression that success of the procedure depends on a flexed neck. Intrathecal puncture is performed at L3-4/L4-5 interspace; the patient simply needs to arch his/her back quite like the back of a crouched cat. The assistant should support the patient at the shoulders and not exert pressure on the neck.The authors state in the results that "patients in the lateral-flexed group did not require redirection of needles." However, the table in their article depicts that 20 patients required needle redirection in the lateral-flexed group. Based on these considerations, I am not convinced that the findings of dr. Biswas *et al*. could challenge the clinical usefulness of the flexed position for performance of spinal anaesthesia.

texts.full_text: eceive 40 mg/day citalopram and placebo for 1 week each, separated by a 1-week washout period. Daily standard alcoholic drinks (13.6 gm ethanol), nonalcoholic drinks, and tobacco use were recorded; evening urine samples were taken; and interest, desire, craving, and liking for alcohol were rated. Medical status, depression, and anxiety were assessed weekly, but no other treatment or advice was given. Daily alcoholic drinks significantly decreased during citalopram treatment (mean +/- SEM = 4.6 +/- 0.6) compared with placebo (5.7 +/- 0.8; p = 0.01), and the average decrease was 17.5%. Percentage of days abstinent increased during citalopram administration (27.7% +/- 5.7%) compared with placebo (15.5% +/- 3.7%; p less than 0.01). Citalopram decreased interest, desire, craving, and liking for alcohol (all p less than 0.05). There was clear internal validation of these measures in that variations in each correlated with alcohol consumption (all r greater than 0.5, p less than 0.05). Nonalcoholic drinks, self-reports of cigarettes smoked (daily smokers), and body weight did not change significantly. In experimental bar sessions, after the citalopram and placebo periods, subjects were required to consume as many of 18 minidrinks as possible (equivalent to six standard drinks) at 5-minute intervals. Subjects rated their desire for alcohol, intoxication, and mood. Citalopram had no significant effects on the desirability of alcohol or subjective feelings of intoxication. The findings indicate that serotonin uptake inhibitors may act by decreasing the urge to drink and the reinforcing effects of alcohol. d elements that contain "Akacos", ignoring accents, your XPath query could look like this: ### Using `translate` in XPATH: ```xpath //p[contains(translate(normalize-space(.), 'ÁÉÍÓÚáéíóú', 'AEIOUaeiou'), translate('Akácoss u.', ' Á É Í Ô Ü á é í ô ü ú',' AE IO U ae io ue'))] ``` ### Explanation: - `normalize-space(.)` removes leading and trailing whitespace from the text content of each `

` element. - The first argument to both instances of the innermost translate function is what you are comparing: `.`, which refers to all characters in your node's string value, or `'Akácoss u.'`. - In this case: ```xpath 'ÁÉÍÓÚáéíóú' -> Characters with 'AEIOUaeiou'- Corresponding unaccented version of the above set (or any other character you want to map it into). ``` - The `contains` function then checks if, after translating both strings by removing or normalizing characters in this way: - Does one string contain another? ### Full XPath Example for Your XML Document: ```xpath //p[contains(translate(normalize-space(.), 'ÁÉÍÓÚáéíóú', 'AEIOUaeiou'), translate('Akacos u.', '', ''))] ``` This will select any `

` elements that have the text "Akacoss" (or variations with accents) within them, treating accented and unaccented characters as equivalent. Note: If you are working in a more complex environment or if performance is an issue to large documents/documents being searched frequently for various strings, consider using XSLT transformations which can offer handling of such normalization tasks outside the XPath query itself.

texts.full_text: y 'few' I exclude most of the people in quant jobs as well. A huge portion of these models is garbage, and this has a far bigger impact than a small bug that is able to slip through unnoticed (after all, results are always subject to interpretation the end user - see 2). Here is what I believe are good points to start with: Introduce version control. If you are here, I do not need to tell you why. But quants don't come here. Unit tests, at least on a per-script level. Have them setup a test environment and then run a test to check whether results are still good. Minimum requirement is to have a set of test-data and have scripts checked with that to see if the results are consistent with previous results to avoid flying blind. I've seen people modifying code and test it on a new set of data, without any clue whether those numbers make sense. Try to avoid that at all costs; at least have a basic idea where you stand. Code reviews and a common code base. Instead of having everybody hacking together individual code pieces that have been pasted from Matlab tutorials, try to get everybody to use a common code base and discuss code they commit with others. This has several advantages: Your code is understood by more team members. This is important, as code is often used interactively. Code is used more, and by more people, which not only saves time, but the next point as well. The bugs are more likely to be found when the code produces results that are unexpected. people will begin to add some comments, at least in my experience. People asking questions are just a waste of time, so give them a chance to figure it out themselves. Get rid of Excel as much as possible. Winter, filed a wrongful death action against the relators, alleging that their negligence in treating Mrs. Winter's pressure ulcer caused her death. The relators moved to dismiss the action, arguing that the intervenor had failed to comply with the requirements of Nebraska's Medical Malpractice Act (the Act). Specifically, the relators argued that the intervenor had not obtained an affidavit from a qualified expert witness stating that there was a reasonable basis to believe that the relators had committed malpractice. The district court denied the relators' motion to dismiss, finding that the intervenor had complied with the Act's requirements. The relators then filed a petition for writ of certiorari with this court, seeking review of the district court's order. On appeal, the relators argue that the district court erred in denying their motion to dismiss because the intervenor's expert affidavit was insufficient to establish a reasonable basis to believe that they had committed malpractice. We disagree. Under the Act, a plaintiff in a medical malpractice action must file an affidavit with the complaint stating that there is a reasonable basis to believe that the defendant has committed malpractice. Neb. Rev. Stat. § 44-2853(1). The affidavit must be signed by a licensed physician who is knowledgeable in the relevant medical area and who has reviewed the pertinent medical records. Id. In this case, the intervenor filed an affidavit from Dr. Susan Smith, a board-certified dermatologist with over 20 years of experience in treating pressure ulcers. Dr. Smith reviewed Mrs. Winter's medical records and stated that, in her opinion, the relators had breached the standard of care in treating Mrs. Winter's pressure ulcer. View slideshow of images above Watch the Did-You-Know slideshow Follow @wiseGEEK ANile monitor lizard, also known as Varanus niloticus, is a small species of reptiles that belong to the family Varanidae. These impressive reptiles can grow up to 2 metres (6.5 feet) in length and weigh between 4-10 kilograms (9-22 pounds) for males while females are slightly larger but equally intimidating with their sharp teeth, strong jaws, and long, muscular tails which they use as a weapon or for balance during high speeds sprints through the terrain. Aside from its native Africa, where it thrives in varied environments such as deserts, savannas, forests, and even human-populated areas if food sources are scarce; their population has also been expanding into areas outside of their natural habitat due to accidental or intentional introductions by humans - especially Australia. Instead of using clipPath for masking, try bitmap masking (it's a bit more complicated that clipPath, but it gives a clear edges for the star). I have modified your code to use bitmap masking instead of clip path. canvas.drawBitmap(backBitmap, 0, 0, q); q.setXfermode(new PorterDuffXfermode(PorterDuff.Mode.DST_IN)); canvas.drawBitmap(starBitmap, 0, 0, q); q.setXfermode(null); } private void initialDraw() { starPaint.setStyle(Paint.Style.FILL_AND_STROKE); starPaint.setAntiAlias(true); minDim = Math.min(this.getWidth() - this.getPaddingLeft() - this.getPaddingRight(), this.getHeight() - this.getPaddingTop() - this.getPaddingBottom()); bigHypot = (minDim / Math.cos(Math.toRadians(18))); bigB = minDim; bigA = Math.tan(Math.toRadians(18)) * bigB; littleHypot = bigHypot / (2 + Math.cos(Math.toRadians(72)) + Math.cos(Math.toRadians(72))); littleA = Math.cos(Math.toRadians(72)) * littleHypot; texts.full_text: outh Korea. With a background in history, math, engineering and anthropology, he helped launch Habitat for Humanity in the 1970s. He has been on the international board of directors since 1995. In 1984, as national chairman for the Fellowship of Reconciliation (FOR), he helped lead a delegation to Nicaragua during the war there between the Sandinistas and the Contras. That experience impacted him to lead many such groups on dozens of trips to conflict zones in Central America, the Middle East and other parts of the world. In 2003 he helped to launch the All Our Children campaign by which thousands of people in U.S. churches and mosques have provided medicine for Iraqi children. Mosley is a writer and lectures in churches, universities and in other settings. He is author of books titled "With Our Own Eyes" and "Faith Beyond Borders." He and his wife, Carolyn Mosley, are founding members of the Jubilee Partners community in Georgia, which has hostedthousands of visitors from around the world to experience communal living based on Christian principles. The community is known for its focus on hospitality, sustainability, peacebuilding initiatives, and interfaith dialogue. Donald's work with Habitat for Humanity reflects his commitment not only to building physical homes but also fostering a sense of home through social engagement and mutual support within communities worldwide. His experiences in conflict zones have further shaped his approach towards promoting reconciliation efforts across different cultures and faiths. Through the All Our Children campaign, Mosley has demonstrated how religious institutions can come together for humanitarian causes beyond their immediate congregations, emphasizing unity over division during times of crisis such as war or natural disasters. texts.full_text: ew York State and New England dairy farmers who arrived in the Big Apple with one goal. The farmers came to personally give thanks to New Yorkers for their loyalty and support during the first-ever Cabot Dairy Farmer Gratitude Tour. Coach buses, wrapped in signature Cabot plaid, traveled throughout Manhattan and Brooklyn dropping off the farmers at their food market posts, where shoppers sampled an array of award-winning cheddars. The farmers also encouraged passersby on the street to jot down “Why I Love Farmers” (or “Why I Love Cows”) and snap a shot with a farmer to enter an Instagram contest. The winner would receive a 3-day Farm Stay Vacation at Liberty Hill Farm in Vermont. The Farmer’s Gratitude Tour connected New Yorkers with the people responsible for the food on their plates, offering a glimpse into the hard work and dedication that goes into producing the dairy products they enjoy daily. The event not only highlighted the importance of supporting local agriculture but also fostered a deeper understanding and appreciation for the role of dairy farmers in their community. Throughout the day, the farmers engaged with New Yorkers, sharing stories about their farms, the challenges they face, and the pride they take in providing high-quality dairy products. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, with many participants expressing gratitude for the opportunity to meet the people behind their food. The Instagram contest was a hit, with participants capturing heartfelt moments between farmers and city dwellers. The photos showcased the genuine connection that can be formed between consumers and producers of food, emphasizing the importance of transparency and direct interaction in building trust and understanding.
